page: Amazon Web Services i2.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores with hyper-threading) operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The processor includes a 25 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AES instructions. The instance is equipped with 122.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.62 GB per core.

A key feature of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of 3200 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It does not include any GPU accelerators. Networking capabilities are defined by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals average performance across several metrics, alongside some notable limitations. The instance achieves average results in stress-ng single-core (1623.49) and multi-core (14125.68) CPU tests, as well as in PassMark CPU (16683.51) and database operations (6516.2). However, single-threaded performance is a bottleneck, as evidenced by poor Geekbench single-core (532.0) and text processing (1080.0) scores, and poor single-threaded bzip3 decompression (7.37 GB/sec). Large block memory write bandwidth is also weak at 18.74 GB/sec.

Given its hardware profile, the i2.4xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-capacity local NVMe storage combined with moderate memory density, avoiding the need for external storage attachments. It is suited for database operations, Redis caching (achieving an average of 6.04 million SET operations per second), static web serving, and general storage-intensive tasks. It is less suitable for modern LLM inference or compute-heavy single-threaded applications due to its older CPU architecture and weak single-core benchmark results.
description: Amazon Web Services i2.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor. It delivers 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores) running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 122.0 GB of memory and a substantial 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. While it lacks GPU capabilities and has a modest 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, its hardware profile is tailored for data-intensive tasks. Benchmark results show average multi-core CPU performance and database operations, though single-core performance and large-block memory write speeds are weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for localized database deployments, Redis caching, and static web serving where high-capacity local storage is required, but it is not recommended for single-threaded compute-bound workloads or modern machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i2.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 122.0 GB of RAM, and 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database workloads and static web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i2.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 122 GB RAM, and 3200 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSDs and balanced memory for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor
122.0 GB of system memory providing 7.62 GB per core
3200 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET transactions
Weak single-core CPU and large-block memory write speeds
Best suited for local databases, caching, and static web serving
